title: poa pratensis var. transnominata
intro: Poa pratensis var. transnominata, also known as the Kentucky bluegrass, is a subspecies of the perennial grass Poa pratensis. It is used in herbal medicine, where it is valued for its diuretic and antiseptic properties, helping to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
